titleStmt.title: Copy of a letter from Serjeant Alex Sullivan to George Gavan Duffy, 4 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Serjeant Alex Sullivan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a copy of a letter from Serjeant Alex Sullivan, Rosscarbery, Co. Cork to Gavan Duffy (1882-1951), in which he conveys his condolences on the execution of Roger Casement (1864–1916). States that 'these asses (the English) can learn nothing. They have made a martyr most stupidly, while abstaining from their hysterical performance would have been a good move for their own ends.'George Gavan Duffy was an Irish politician, barrister and judge. He unsuccessfully defended Roger Casement at his trial for high treason after the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Letter from Serjeant Sullivan to G. Gavan Duffy. Rosscarberry Co. Cork. 4.8.16. My Dear Gorge, I am exceedingly sorry for the disappointment
and worry that you have suffered. These asses can learn
nothing. They have made a 'martyr' most stupidly
while abstaining from their hysterical performance
wd. have been a good move for their own ends. Come down to me and have some rest and
relaxation. Brooding over this business will do you
no good ..... I am at last resting ... I shall do
no work for a month at least. Kindest regards to you both
Yrs .. Alex
